item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This manages to be one of the truly unsettling and unnerving SCPs which is something I appreciate due to its rarity on the site.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked the beginning. I thought when it communicated that was a bit lame. The concept of a painting with veins and bones is really interesting—the thought of being trapped in paint, completely still and unable to communicate was creepy too. When it talked that sort of destroyed the intrigue for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this SCP precisely because it made me think more about the "living painting" trope that I ever cared to before. The flat skeleton in the x-rays sold me on it. +1'd.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kalinin's comments in the first comment completely sum up my feelings on this SCP

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
scp itself is great, the "interview" type thing not so much

added an it in the "implying that is a living organism". didn't think that was a purposeful mistake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Upvoted because the idea and execution are great, and I agree it tails off a little bit even now, but I don't know where you'd take it instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Mmmm… I really do like the concept, but it still doesn't have oomph.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Okay, it's done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Personally, I'd either change the reference from Mary Magdalene or alter that quote. Especially if you're claiming it to be Eastern Orthodox.

This is the only point of this item that I have any issue with. It's otherwise very well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You're right. Apparently, the Eastern Orthodox tradition does not merge Mary Magdalene and the possibly distinct Mary of Bethany into one 'composite Mary' as the Catholic church often does. Blame it on my ignorance of Eastern theology.

I'm not sure if I should change it, I think I'm just going to plead artistic license here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This has been improved in all the right ways. Unlike those above, I think the addendum adds just the right amount of story.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know this isn't really the hinge from which the skip pivots upon, but Mary Magdalene and the prostitute who washes Jesus' feet are not the same person. It's kind of a common mistake made by a lot of people, but it being in this kind of ruins it for me? Still, neutral vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice to see you changed it. +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hrm… Really mixed on this. Like others have said, it's short on the hook. There's just not enough seen into the backstory to really pull it off, even though everyhting else is done very well. It just needs to tie up better, and i'd upvote without hesitation. As is, it feels like a story half done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This actually didn't have anything to do with Christianity at all when I first wrote it. It was initially a simple painting of a woman that was owned by a nervous, unstable man. But I loved the melancholy expression on the picture I chose, so I changed the story a little so it would fit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I always get nervous when I see SCPs with blatant Christian imagery but this is actually quite good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fourth'd.

I really like the concept though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1

I like it. I think there's enough hints at something deeper to be enjoyable and interesting. I liked the hints at breaking the chastity bond, that she thinks this is punishment for that, and that the people who imprisoned her worshipped something other than God, while claiming to worship God.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Looks like he changed it, but I didn't mind it. I saw it more as a legitimate act of an extremely frustrated, trapped soul than "creepy." I mean, if you were trapped, wouldn't you at least try stubborn repetition once? We've all done it, especially as children. "Take me out for ice cream. ice cream. ice cream. ice cream. ice cream. ice cream. ice cream. ice cream. ice cream. ice cream!"

It showed a soul who doesn't understand her circumstances very well and has a certain young naivety.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You guys are right, that repitition thing is pretty cliche. I changed it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was ready to upvote until that last addendum. Then it was a downvote.
Repeating something creepy over and over hasn't worked since The Shining.
Addendum at issue has been removed.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It started out really well, very interesting concept and item. But it does trail off at the end. You lost coherence as you went. You have a great idea, but you don't have a hook. I'm no voting for now, but if it is still up later on today, I will come back, read again, and then vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely some potential here, but its just kind of uninteresting as is. No vote

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really want to like this, I really do, but it just falls flat. Those first messages are properly creepy, the entire concept is delightful, but trailing off into, as Rioghail says, "standard 'crazy repetition'" just makes it fall down. I mean, surely suddenly having the ability to communicate help keep her slightly more sane?

So maybe have a bit of a think about how being able to communicate has changed her. About how she's no longer in the monastery, but in a scientific facility. About how she came to be in the icon, and how she might want to tell her story - if she can.

Because I really do want to like this - hell, I want to love this. I love the idea. But I need to have it more than just "She's trapped in a painting and it's ka-razy!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
3rd'd

Edit: Unimpressed with the changes. My no-vote stands.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This I exactly why I no-voted. It's good in writing and concept and such, but the end was generic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The starting idea is good, and the description is the sort of thing I can get behind, but it just sort of trails off at the end. The initial messages (and the method they are transmitted in) are rather interesting, but it's disappointing that you decided to have the painting dissolve into standard 'crazy repetition' rather than expand on what it had already said, give us a little more understanding of what happened.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Was looking for a hook, let down at the end. As Kalin stated, it's just not interesting enough to be it's own hook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As noted in the forums, I'm just not digging where you end up going with the original concept of an anatomical figure trapped in a painting. I feel in general that it's an interesting starting concept that ends up being played disappointingly straight.
